description: Xilinx ZCU102 (Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, XCZU9EG) with ADI MxFE FMC daughter cards. Profiles in this catalog cover validated MxFE operating points; profile data (manifests + patches + upstream pristine subtrees) lives in each consuming project under its own `socks.json::adi.no_os_subtree` and `socks.json::adi.hdl_subtree`. The catalog is name-only — it lists which profiles are known + their high-level operating points + their hardware-validation status, NOT the patch bytes.
fmc_daughter_cards_supported: ad9081-fmca-ebz (reference design; some profiles work on AD9986-FMCA via chip-ID acceptance patch)

6144-jesd204b-m8l4.description: 61.44-MHz PL link clock; lane rate 2.4576 Gbps; JESD204B 8B/10B encoding, M=8 / L=4 / S=1 / NP=16 (RX = 4 ADC RF inputs each emitted as I/Q). HMC7044 PLL2 at 2.94912 GHz from a 122.88 MHz VCXO; SYSREF at 1.92 MHz LMFC.
6144-jesd204b-m8l4.validation_summary: Manual hardware re-validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware. The consuming project reached all five UART pass markers for the 6144-jesd204b-m8l4 profile.

24576-jesd204b-m8l4.description: PL link clock at 245.76 MHz with lane rate 9.8304 Gbps, JESD204B 8B/10B encoding, M=8 / L=4 / S=1 / NP=16 (RX = 4 ADC RF inputs each emitted as I/Q). HMC7044 PLL2 at 2.94912 GHz from a 122.88 MHz VCXO; device refclk 491.52 MHz; FPGA GT refclk 491.52 MHz; ADXCVR exports refclk/2 as the 245.76 MHz link/device clock; SYSREF at 1.92 MHz.
24576-jesd204b-m8l4.validation_summary: Hardware validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware for the 24576-jesd204b-m8l4 profile. The consuming project reached all five UART pass markers at lane rate / 40 = 245.760 MHz.

6144-l1-clean-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.description: GPS-L1 clean-NCO profile: 61.44 MHz RX PL link clock + 122.88 MSPS TX JESD sample rate, 2.4576 Gbps lanes both directions. Asymmetric M/L: RX M=8/L=4 (mode 10) at 61.44 MSPS, TX M=8/L=8 (mode 15) at 122.88 MSPS. AD9986 converter clocks at 3.93216 GHz make GPS L1 (1575.42 MHz) an exact rational fraction of converter rate (26257/65536), giving the 48-bit main-NCO FTW 32 trailing binary zeros and 0 Hz residual carrier-frequency error. The asymmetric TX (mode 15) was forced by pyadi-jif: the AD9986 cannot sustain TX at M=8/L=4 / 61.44 MSPS while the DAC clock is 3.93216 GHz at 2.4576 Gbps lanes; mode 15 M=8/L=8 / 122.88 MSPS is the only valid TX choice.
6144-l1-clean-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.asymmetric_rx_tx_note: Different M/L between RX and TX. RX runs M=8/L=4 mode 10 at 61.44 MSPS; TX runs M=8/L=8 mode 15 at 122.88 MSPS. Both share the same 2.4576-Gbps lane-rate budget (TX uses twice the number of lanes to deliver twice the sample rate).
6144-l1-clean-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.validation_summary: Hardware validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware. Reached all five UART pass markers; both RX and TX JESD links train to DATA at lane rate / 40 = 61.440 MHz with SYSREF aligned. UART evidence captured tx_dac=122 875 976 Hz, rx_adc=61 437 988 Hz.

24576-clean-l1-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l4.description: GPS-L1 clean-NCO 245.76-MHz PL link profile; lanes 9.8304 Gbps, symmetric RX/TX M=8/L=4 (RX mode 10, TX mode 9), 3.93216 GHz converter clocks (same clean GPS-L1 NCO ratio as 6144-l1-clean). Symmetric variant of the same clean-L1 NCO architecture at 4x the link clock; not bitstream-compatible with 6144-l1-clean (different fabric clocking). Use this when the application needs the wider PL substrate bandwidth (~120 MHz at Nyquist) and can absorb the higher SerDes lane rate.
24576-clean-l1-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l4.validation_summary: Hardware validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware. Reached all five UART pass markers; both RX and TX JESD links train to DATA at lane rate / 40 = 245.760 MHz with SYSREF aligned. UART evidence captured tx_dac=245 755 004 Hz, rx_adc=245 753 479 Hz.

6144-quad-band-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.description: Quad-band GPS L5 / L2 / L1 + Iridium ALT-NAV simultaneous-reception profile. Reuses the 6144-l1-clean HDL/JESD shape (61.44 MHz RX PL link, 122.88 MSPS TX, 2.4576 Gbps lanes, asymmetric RX M=8/L=4 mode 10 / TX M=8/L=8 mode 15) but overrides the no-OS RX DDC crossbar + per-FDDC NCO retargeting to emit four GPS-band virtual converters simultaneously. All four band NCOs land at exact rational fractions of the 3.93216 GHz converter clock (33-43 trailing binary zeros, 0 Hz residual). TX path is identical to 6144-l1-clean's TX (single-band L1 transmit). This is the dual-frequency-and-beyond foundation for L1+L2 ionosphere removal and L5/Iridium augmentation.
6144-quad-band-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.asymmetric_rx_tx_note: Same asymmetric M/L shape as 6144-l1-clean: RX M=8/L=4 mode 10 at 61.44 MSPS; TX M=8/L=8 mode 15 at 122.88 MSPS. Same 2.4576-Gbps lane-rate budget.
6144-quad-band-jesd204b-rxm8l4-txm8l8.validation_summary: Hardware validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware per the consuming project's commit verification trailer (make PASS, hil_run.py PASS, adi_profile_apply.py PASS, pyadi-jif PASS). Structured handback's gates[] array did not enumerate all five UART markers individually, but the adi_profile_apply + hil_run round-trip exercised the full hardware-validation path. Treat as validated; per-band physical-mapping verification is a follow-on RX-data-path step (cross-band correlation + spectrum capture) before claiming the FDDC-to-physical-ADC routing in vivo.

sources_when_picking_a_new_profile.purpose: ADI no-OS reference firmware for AD9081/AD9986; vendored as pristine + patch series in consuming projects.
ADI HDL reference designs (ad9081_fmca_ebz, etc.); vendored as pristine + per-profile patch series in consuming projects.
Cross-check authoritative source for M/L/S/NP combos and crossbar / converter-select values per profile.
Enumeration of FDDC virtual converter IDs (FDDC0_I = 0, FDDC0_Q = 1, FDDC1_I = 2, FDDC1_Q = 3, FDDC2_I = 4, ..., FDDC5_Q = 11).
Default routing of ADC pairs to FDDC virtual converters (ADC0 → FDDC0/2, ADC1 → FDDC1/3, ADC2 → FDDC4/6, ADC3 → FDDC5/7).

Per-profile array selecting which FDDCs are emitted on which JESD virtual converters. 8-element array maps VC[0..7] -> FDDC enum values; cross-reference with the API enum to determine VC-to-physical-ADC mapping.

TX-side equivalents (AD9081_TX_JESD_M/L/S/NP, AD9081_TX_LOGICAL_LANE_MAPPING, AD9081_TX_DAC_CHAN_CROSSBAR, AD9081_TX_DAC_MAIN_CHAN_CROSSBAR). The pinned no-OS app at b45866d does NOT have a TX_LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT analog to RX; TX physical mapping requires a TX-data-path validation rather than inference from link-up markers alone.
Per-profile HMC7044 PLL2 + output-divider + SYSREF settings for the device + FPGA refclks.

methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.summary: Three-layer derivation: (1) read the no-OS app's per-profile *_LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT array; (2) cross-reference with the AD9081 API enum (adi_ad9081_adc_fine_ddc_converter_e for RX, equivalent DAC enum for TX); (3) cross-reference with the API's default crossbar datapath. (4) Cross-check the result against ADI Linux DTS overlay for the same M/L/S/NP combo.
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.rx_status: Working RX methodology validated for the 6144-jesd204b-m8l4 profile (FDDC0/1/4/5 → ADC0/1/2/3).
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.tx_status: TX methodology only partially proven from no-OS source. The pinned no-OS app has no TX LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT analog to RX; TX VC-to-DAC physical mapping requires a TX-data-path validation step (e.g., DAC output spectrum capture + per-channel correlation) before claiming a definitive mapping.
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.deferred_field_shape: When the methodology is run-able but the result is unproven, populate the manifest's operating_point.<rx|tx>.physical_mapping with status='methodology-only' + source_files[] + crossbar_note + observed values, and explicitly disclaim VC-to-physical mapping. Do NOT leave the field empty / null / fabricated.

catalog_authoring_rules.genericization: This catalog file is in the SOCKS skill repo (~/.claude/skills/socks/references/boards/zcu102/) and is shared by every consuming project on this board. Do NOT include project-specific paths, workspace-rooted paths, validated_evidence fields, first_validated_thread fields, or any other consuming-project provenance. Provenance lives in each consuming project's findings docs and links upward to a catalog entry by name only.
catalog_authoring_rules.validated_date_lifecycle: Set validated_date to a real ISO date only after a real hardware run on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware reaches all five UART pass markers. Software-only checks (patches apply, JSON parses, fingerprint scan clean) are NOT sufficient to set validated_date — they prove the patch system is structurally correct, not that the resulting configuration trains a JESD link in vivo.
catalog_authoring_rules.profile_addition: New profiles authored in any consuming project are added here by name + operating_point summary + uart_pass_markers + patch_layout_contract. The catalog does NOT carry the patch bytes themselves — those live in each consuming project's own ADI subtree per its socks.json::adi configuration.
